nce is an opportunity for users and researchers from industry, academia, and government to get together and present their work to the Ptolemy community. It is typically held every two years. The 4th Biennial Ptolemy Miniconference was held on March

iennial Ptolemy Miniconference was held on March 22-23, 2001, at the Claremont Hotel in Berkeley, with 93 attendees from 44 organizations worldwide. Pdf versions of all of the talks are posted in Program Audience for Chamberlain's talk on Friday mor
